LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERATING ENGINEERS MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE PETITIONER HAS NOT SHOWN THE EXISTENCE OF AN ENFORCEABLE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE THAT COVERS HIS CLAIM. PREVAILING PARTY IS PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS APPROVED AS TO FORM AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERATING ENGINEERS STATIONARY ENGINEERS, LOCAL 39'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. THE UNION BEARS THE BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT JASON FONG'S DISPUTE REGARDING HIS RELEASE FROM EMPLOYMENT IS ARBITRABLE AND IT FAILED TO DO SO. PARAGRAPH 44 OF THE MOU PROVIDES THAT "PERMANENT EMPLOYEES OR EMPLOYEES WHO HAVE SATISFACTORILY COMPLETED THE PROBATIONARY PERIOD MAY" ARBITRATE THEIR RELEASE FROM EMPLOYMENT. EVEN IF THE COURT CONSIDERED THE APPRENTICE AGREEMENT SUBMITTED WITH THE REPLY PAPERS, THE UNION HAS NOT PROVIDED ANY EVIDENCE THAT MR. FONG WAS EITHER A PERMANENT EMPLOYEE OR THAT HE SATISFACTORILY COMPLETED A PROBATIONARY PERIOD. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gary P. Provencher, Esq., Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld, Telephone 916-443-6600, appearing for Petitioner IUOE Stationary Engineers Local 39;
rose Darling, Esq., Deput City Attorney CCSF, Telephone 415-554-3959, appearing for Defendant
CCSF;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERATING ENGINEERS Motion To Compel Arbitration

The motion to compel arbitration is denied on the grounds that the Petitioner has not shown the existence of an enforceable agreement to arbitrate that covers his claim. Prevailing party is prepare and submit a proposed order that is approved as to form and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gary Provencher, Esq., Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld, Telephone 510-337-1001, appearing for Petitioner; (CourtCall)
Erick A Rapoport, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, Telephone 415-554-3950, appearing for Defendant;

Ruling - ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Petitioner International Union of Operating Engineers Stationary Engineers, Local 39's petition to compel arbitration is denied. The Union bears the burden of showing that Jason Fong's dispute regarding his release from employment is arbitrable and it failed to do so. Paragraph 44 of the MOU provides that "permanent employees or employees who have satisfactorily completed the probationary period may" arbitrate their release from employment. Even if the court considered the Apprentice Agreement submitted with the reply papers, the Union has not provided any evidence that Mr. Fong was either a permanent employee or that he satisfactorily completed a probationary period. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)
